message:
robot: register auto-restart-stuck in schema, capabilities, and docs (bd-297ol)

Add the new health-restart-stuck command to all three robot mode
discovery registries so agents can find it via --robot-capabilities,
--robot-schema, and --robot-docs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-297mu","title":"Task: Add robot preflight command using DCG","description":"## Overview
Expose a robot-first command to preflight an arbitrary shell command via DCG.

## Proposed Interface
- `ntm --robot-guard --cmd "rm -rf /tmp"`
- Output: {success, allowed, reason, dcg_version}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns structured JSON with allow/deny + rationale
- Fails gracefully if dcg missing
- No command execution; preflight only","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-29cp6","title":"Feature: Encryption-at-rest for NTM artifacts (optional)","description":"## Summary
Provide an optional encryption layer for persisted NTM artifacts (prompt history, event logs, archives) for users who want stronger guarantees than redaction alone.

## Design Sketch
- Config: `[encryption] enabled=true` + key source:
  - explicit key in config (discouraged)
  - env var
  - OS keychain integration (future)
- Use standard library crypto (AES-GCM) with per-file random nonce.

## Scope
- Start with encrypting:
  - prompt history storage
  - event log JSONL
- Leave archives/checkpoints as follow-up (or encrypt bundle contents individually).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Encrypted artifacts are unreadable without key.
- Failure modes are explicit and do not corrupt data.
- Unit tests cover encrypt/decrypt round-trip.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:29:14.948636583Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:29:14.955398485Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29cp6","depends_on_id":"bd-27hn0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29dh","title":"Task: Implement one-click replay","description":"## Purpose
Enable replaying a prompt to a new or existing agent with one action.

## Scope
- Add replay action to history TUI (Enter or 'r' key)
- Options: replay to new agent, replay to specific agent, replay with modifications
- Track replay lineage for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can replay any historical prompt
- Replay spawns new agent or sends to existing
- Can edit prompt before replay
- Replay history tracks parent prompt

## Technical Notes
- Reuse spawn/send logic from existing commands
- Consider prompt templates for common modifications","notes":"Assume existing history storage; focus on UI/UX replay flow only.

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-352n","title":"Task: Add help --minimal / --full switch","description":"## Overview
Replace tiered/proficiency gating with a simple help verbosity switch.

## Deliverables
1. Add --minimal and --full flags to ntm help (or ntm --help).
2. Minimal view shows essential commands only (spawn, send, status, kill, help).
3. Full view shows the complete command set (current behavior).
4. Optional config default: help_verbosity = minimal|full.

## Non-Goals
- No proficiency tiers, gating, or promotion logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Minimal help is concise and stable
- Full help remains unchanged
- No command gating","notes":"Tiered gating removed; this task is the canonical help verbosity switch.

- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-352u","title":"Task: Add timeline persistence for post-session analysis","description":"## Overview
Enable saving timeline data to disk for analysis after session ends.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/state/timeline_persist.go:
   - SaveTimeline(sessionID string, events []AgentEvent) error
   - LoadTimeline(sessionID string) ([]AgentEvent, error)
   - ListTimelines() ([]TimelineInfo, error)

2. Storage format:
   - JSONL file per session: ~/.local/share/ntm/timelines/<session>.jsonl
   - Each line is one AgentEvent as JSON
   - Include header with session metadata

3. Automatic persistence:
   - Save on session end
   - Periodic checkpoint every 5 minutes
   - Recover from checkpoint on crash

4. CLI commands:
   - ntm timeline show <session-id> - view historical timeline
   - ntm timeline list - list available timelines
   - ntm timeline delete <session-id> - remove old timelines

5. Retention policy:
   - Keep last 30 sessions by default
   - Configurable via ntm.yaml
   - Automatic cleanup of old timelines

## Implementation Notes
- Use standard JSON encoding for portability
- Compress old timelines with gzip
- Index by session ID for fast lookup

## Acceptance Criteria
- Timelines persist across NTM restarts
- Can load and view historical timelines
- Automatic cleanup works
- Unit tests for persistence logic","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyCliff","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:33.281526043Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T04:19:39.965551811Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-352u","depends_on_id":"bd-2t7n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-352u","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-35bn4","title":"Feature: Auto-Ensemble Selection - Recommend best preset from question analysis","description":"# Feature: Auto-Ensemble Selection

## Problem Statement
Users face choice paralysis with 9 ensemble presets. They ask:
- Which ensemble is best for my question?
- What's the difference between bug-hunt and root-cause?
- Am I picking the right one?

## Solution
Analyze question text and recommend the best-fit ensemble with explanation (deterministic, rule-based).

## User Experience
```bash
ntm ensemble suggest "What security vulnerabilities exist in the auth system?"
# Output:
# Recommended: safety-risk
# Confidence: HIGH
#
# Reason: matched keywords: security, vulnerabilities → safety-risk
#
# Alternatives:
#   bug-hunt (0.65) - If looking for specific implementation bugs
#   root-cause (0.45) - If investigating known security incident
#
# To run: ntm ensemble spawn safety-risk --question "..."
```

## Piping Support
```bash
# Get just the ID for piping
ntm ensemble suggest --question "What security..." --id-only
# Output: safety-risk

# Pipe to spawn
ntm ensemble spawn $(ntm ensemble suggest --question "..." --id-only) --question "..."
```

## Matching Algorithm (Deterministic)
Pattern-based (no ML) for reliability:

```go
var ensembleKeywords = map[string][]string{
    "project-diagnosis":   {"architecture", "structure", "codebase", "overview", "health"},
    "idea-forge":          {"feature", "improve", "add", "enhance", "brainstorm", "ideas"},
    "spec-critique":       {"spec", "requirements", "api", "interface", "contract"},
    "safety-risk":         {"security", "vulnerability", "threat", "compliance", "risk"},
    "architecture-review": {"design", "pattern", "refactor", "structure", "scalable"},
    "tech-debt-triage":    {"debt", "legacy", "cleanup", "deprecat", "upgrade"},
    "bug-hunt":            {"bug", "error", "fail", "broken", "issue"},
    "root-cause":          {"why", "cause", "investigate", "debug", "trace"},
    "strategic-planning":  {"roadmap", "priority", "plan", "strategy", "quarter"},
}
```

Scoring rules:
- Lowercase + simple stemming (suffix trim)
- Keyword count × weights
- Tie-breaker: preset priority order
- Confidence = normalized score → HIGH/MED/LOW

## Robot API
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-suggest --question="..."
# Returns: {ensemble_id, confidence, reason, alternatives: [...]}
```

## Logging
- Log matched keywords + scores
- Log chosen preset + confidence

## Tests
- Unit: scoring + tie-breakers
- Unit: id-only output
- E2E: suggest + spawn piping
- E2E: robot JSON schema + deterministic ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] suggest command analyzes question text
- [ ] Returns top match with confidence score
- [ ] Shows 2-3 alternatives with scores
- [ ] Explains why each was matched/ranked
- [ ] --id-only flag for piping
- [ ] Robot API returns structured JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:02:50.039679175Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T02:23:19.144079127Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T02:23:19.143356436Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35bn4","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35bn4","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35bn4","depends_on_id":"bd-1kthb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35chh","title":"Test: Unit tests for BeadScanner","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for AllocationCalculator tier calculations, ensuring correct agent allocation based on bead counts.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/allocation_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "testing"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestCalculateTier(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        Tier1Threshold: 400,
        Tier2Threshold: 100,
    }
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    tests := []struct {
        name      string
        beadCount int
        wantTier  int
    }{
        // Tier 1: 400+ beads
        {"tier1_minimum", 400, 1},
        {"tier1_high", 1000, 1},
        {"tier1_exact_boundary", 400, 1},
        
        // Tier 2: 100-399 beads
        {"tier2_minimum", 100, 2},
        {"tier2_maximum", 399, 2},
        {"tier2_middle", 250, 2},
        
        // Tier 3: <100 beads
        {"tier3_maximum", 99, 3},
        {"tier3_minimum", 1, 3},
        {"tier3_zero", 0, 3},
        {"tier3_negative", -1, 3}, // Edge case
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Testing bead count %d, expecting tier %d", tt.beadCount, tt.wantTier)
            got := calc.CalculateTier(tt.beadCount)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantTier, got, 
                "beadCount=%d should be tier %d, got %d", tt.beadCount, tt.wantTier, got)
        })
    }
}

func TestCalculateProjectAllocation(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        Tier1Threshold: 400,
        Tier2Threshold: 100,
        Tier1Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 3, Cod: 2, Gmi: 1},
        Tier2Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 2, Cod: 1, Gmi: 1},
        Tier3Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 1, Cod: 1, Gmi: 0},
    }
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    tests := []struct {
        name       string
        project    ProjectBeadCount
        wantCC     int
        wantCod    int
        wantGmi    int
        wantTotal  int
    }{
        {
            name:      "tier1_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/big", Name: "big", OpenBeads: 500},
            wantCC:    3, wantCod: 2, wantGmi: 1, wantTotal: 6,
        },
        {
            name:      "tier2_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/med", Name: "med", OpenBeads: 200},
            wantCC:    2, wantCod: 1, wantGmi: 1, wantTotal: 4,
        },
        {
            name:      "tier3_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/small", Name: "small", OpenBeads: 50},
            wantCC:    1, wantCod: 1, wantGmi: 0, wantTotal: 2,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Project: %s, Beads: %d", tt.project.Name, tt.project.OpenBeads)
            
            alloc := calc.CalculateProjectAllocation(tt.project)
            
            t.Logf("Result: CC=%d, Cod=%d, Gmi=%d, Total=%d",
                alloc.CCAgents, alloc.CodAgents, alloc.GmiAgents, alloc.TotalAgents)
            
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantCC, alloc.CCAgents, "CC agents mismatch")
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantCod, alloc.CodAgents, "Cod agents mismatch")
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantGmi, alloc.GmiAgents, "Gmi agents mismatch")
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantTotal, alloc.TotalAgents, "Total agents mismatch")
        })
    }
}

func TestCalculateAllocations_Sorting(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := defaultTestConfig()
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    // Input in random order
    projects := []ProjectBeadCount{
        {Path: "/p/small", Name: "small", OpenBeads: 50},
        {Path: "/p/big", Name: "big", OpenBeads: 500},
        {Path: "/p/med", Name: "med", OpenBeads: 200},
    }
    
    t.Logf("Input order: %v", projectNames(projects))
    
    allocations := calc.CalculateAllocations(projects)
    
    // Should be sorted by bead count descending
    require.Len(t, allocations, 3)
    assert.Equal(t, "big", allocations[0].Project.Name, "First should be highest beads")
    assert.Equal(t, "med", allocations[1].Project.Name, "Second should be medium beads")
    assert.Equal(t, "small", allocations[2].Project.Name, "Third should be lowest beads")
    
    t.Logf("Output order: %v (correctly sorted by bead count desc)", 
        allocationNames(allocations))
}

func TestGenerateSwarmPlan(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        SessionsPerType: 2,
        // ... full config
    }
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    projects := []ProjectBeadCount{
        {Path: "/p/a", Name: "a", OpenBeads: 450},
        {Path: "/p/b", Name: "b", OpenBeads: 150},
    }
    
    plan := calc.GenerateSwarmPlan("/test", projects)
    
    t.Logf("Plan created: %s", plan.CreatedAt)
    t.Logf("Allocations: %d", len(plan.Allocations))
    t.Logf("Sessions: %d", len(plan.Sessions))
    t.Logf("Totals: CC=%d, Cod=%d, Gmi=%d", plan.TotalCC, plan.TotalCod, plan.TotalGmi)
    
    assert.NotEmpty(t, plan.Sessions, "Should create sessions")
    assert.Equal(t, "/test", plan.ScanDir)
    
    // Verify session naming
    sessionNames := make([]string, 0)
    for _, s := range plan.Sessions {
        sessionNames = append(sessionNames, s.Name)
        t.Logf("Session: %s (type=%s, panes=%d)", s.Name, s.AgentType, s.PaneCount)
    }
    
    assert.Contains(t, sessionNames, "cc_agents_1")
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- t.Logf() for all input parameters
- t.Logf() for all computed results
- Clear test names describing scenario
- Boundary condition documentation in comments

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tier boundary tests (exact boundaries)
- [ ] All three tier allocations tested
- [ ] Sorting verification
- [ ] Empty input handling
- [ ] SwarmPlan generation validation
- [ ] Session naming verification
- [ ] Pane distribution tests
- [ ] Verbose logging for debugging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:45.807796493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:52:10.924840463Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:52:10.924743640Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35chh","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7y5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35chh","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35e81","title":"Task: Define ensemble config types (synthesis, budget, display)","description":"# Task: Define ensemble config types (synthesis, budget, display)

Provide shared config structs used by presets, CLI, and manager **without duplicating existing types**.

## Changes
- Extend `EnsemblePreset` / `EnsembleConfig` with:
  - DisplayName (human-friendly label)
  - Description
  - Modes ([]ModeRef, id or code)
  - Synthesis (strategy + synthesizer_mode)
  - Budget (per_agent/total/synthesis/context)
  - Cache (context pack cache control)
  - AllowAdvanced (explicit opt-in for advanced tier)
  - AgentDistribution (optional)
  - Tags (optional)
- **Reuse existing `SynthesisConfig` and `BudgetConfig`** in `internal/ensemble/types.go` instead of redefining.
- Add/confirm `ModeRef` type:
  - Accepts `id` or `code`
  - Canonicalizes to mode ID at load time
- Add/confirm `CacheConfig` type (context pack cache control)
- Update JSON/TOML/YAML tags for config files
- Update validation defaults + docstrings

## Files
- internal/ensemble/types.go
- internal/ensemble/types_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Types used consistently across presets/config/manager
- [ ] No duplicate Budget/Synthesis structs
- [ ] JSON/TOML/YAML tags aligned with config files
- [ ] Modes accept id or code and normalize deterministically
- [ ] Tests cover zero/default values + serialization","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:40:32.129240591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:00:19.841508746Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T23:00:19.841428315Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -973,7 +975,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-kjihq","title":"Swarm CLI: Add --output flag for plan JSON export","description":"## Purpose
Add `--output` flag to the `ntm swarm` subcommand for writing the generated plan to a JSON file.

**NOTE**: This is a SUBCOMMAND flag for `ntm swarm`, NOT a top-level robot flag.

## Command Interface

```bash
# Subcommand usage (NOT robot mode)
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/projects --output=plan.json
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/projects --dry-run --output=plan.json

# Robot mode for querying swarm state is separate (see bd-3cdsi)
ntm --robot-snapshot  # Includes swarm state in unified output
```

## Implementation Details

```go
// In cmd/ntm/swarm.go - subcommand flag, not root robot flag
var outputPath string

func init() {
    swarmCmd.Flags().StringVar(&outputPath, "output", "",
        "Write swarm plan to JSON file (optional)")
}

func runSwarm(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // ... generate plan ...
    
    if outputPath != "" {
        if err := writePlanToFile(plan, outputPath); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("write plan: %w", err)
        }
        slog.Info("plan written", "path", outputPath)
    }
    return nil
}

func writePlanToFile(plan *swarm.SwarmPlan, path string) error {
    dir := filepath.Dir(path)
    if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("create directory: %w", err)
    }
    
    data, err := json.MarshalIndent(plan, "", "  ")
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("marshal plan: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Atomic write
    tmpPath := path + ".tmp"
    if err := os.WriteFile(tmpPath, data, 0644); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    return os.Rename(tmpPath, path)
}
```

## Output Format
```json
{
  "created_at": "2026-01-22T10:30:00Z",
  "scan_dir": "/home/user/projects",
  "allocations": [...],
  "total_cc": 10,
  "total_cod": 8,
  "total_gmi": 6,
  "sessions": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flag is on swarm subcommand (not root)
- [ ] Works with --dry-run
- [ ] Atomic write via temp file
- [ ] Parent directory auto-creation
- [ ] Clear error messages","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletEagle","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:27.275998902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T22:09:42.468830388Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T22:09:42.468762612Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: ntm swarm --output writes SwarmPlan JSON (atomic) + unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kjihq","depends_on_id":"bd-2pgo6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-kjihq","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-kk81t","title":"Implement recommendation generator","description":"Implement recommendation generator for diagnosed issues.

## Recommendations per Status

### rate_limited
- action: wait_or_caam
- reason: 'Output contains rate limit pattern'
- Parse reset_time if available
- auto_fixable: false (requires human decision)

### unresponsive
- action: kill_restart
- reason: 'No output for N seconds, process state: X'
- auto_fixable: true
- fix_command: 'ntm --robot-restart-pane <session> <pane>'

### crashed
- action: restart_cc
- reason: 'No agent process' or 'Zombie process'
- auto_fixable: true
- fix_command: 'ntm --robot-restart-pane <session> <pane>'

## Output Format

Each recommendation includes:
- pane: int
- status: string
- action: string
- reason: string (human readable)
- auto_fixable: bool
- fix_command: string (if auto_fixable)
- Additional context (reset_time, etc.)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:27:33.219751841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.657219571Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.657174857Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kk81t","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-krqz","title":"Add ntm health --auto-restart-stuck - detect and restart agents that appear stuck (no output for N minutes)","description":"Add ntm health --auto-restart-stuck - detect and restart agents that appear stuck (no output for N minutes).

## Problem Being Solved

Agents sometimes hang or get stuck. Currently requires manual detection and restart.

## Command Design

```bash
ntm health SESSION --auto-restart-stuck [--threshold DURATION] [--dry-run]
```

### Behavior
1. Monitors all agent panes for output activity
2. Detects panes with no output for threshold duration
3. Automatically restarts stuck panes

### Detection Logic
- Track last output timestamp per pane
- Pane is 'stuck' if no output for threshold (default: 5m)
- Ignore panes that are intentionally idle (awaiting user input)

### Options
- --threshold: Duration before considering stuck (default: 5m)
- --dry-run: Report stuck panes without restarting

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-health-restart-stuck=SESSION [--threshold=DURATION]

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "session": "my_session",
  "stuck_panes": [3, 5],
  "restarted": [3, 5],
  "threshold": "5m"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. --auto-restart-stuck flag added to ntm health command
2. Detects panes with no output for threshold duration
3. Default threshold is 5 minutes
4. --threshold flag allows custom duration
5. --dry-run reports without restarting
6. Restart uses existing --robot-restart-pane logic
7. Robot mode returns JSON with stuck/restarted panes

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/health_test.go
- Test stuck detection logic
- Test threshold parsing

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-AUTO-RESTART] Auto-restart Stuck Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-AUTO-RESTART]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing --auto-restart-stuck"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. ntm health --auto-restart-stuck detects stuck agents
2. No output for N minutes triggers detection
3. Configurable threshold (default 5 minutes)
4. Automatic restart with confirmation option
5. Logging of restart events
6. Unit tests for detection logic
7. E2E test with simulated stuck agent","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-ktyql","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Env Info","description":"E2E tests for Robot Env Info with actual sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Info Retrieval
Spawn session with --cc=3, verify tmux.binary_path, tmux.version, window_index, agent_pane_start

### Scenario 2: Structure Accuracy
Spawn session with 5 agents, verify total_agent_panes, agent_pane_end, control_pane

### Scenario 3: Targeting Examples Work
Get --robot-env output, parse example_agent_pane, use target to send keys, verify delivery

### Scenario 4: Shell Detection
Verify shell.type detection (bash/zsh), check tmux_plugin_detected

### Scenario 5: Timing Values
Verify all timing values present and reasonable (100ms for ctrl_c_gap, etc.)

### Scenario 6: Config Override
Set custom timing in config, verify custom values reflected in --robot-env

## Logging Requirements
Each test logs: test name, spawn_args, full env_response JSON, verification outputs

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 6 test scenarios implemented as bash scripts
2. Each scenario logs full --robot-env JSON response
3. Tests verify structure matches expected schema
4. Shell detection works for bash and zsh
5. Timing values are within reasonable ranges
6. Config overrides properly reflected
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. All 6 test scenarios pass
2. Test logs follow JSON format specification

4. Tests are idempotent (can run multiple times)
5. Test duration under 3 minutes total
6. Each scenario logs setup/execute/verify/result
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, 1 on any failure
8.
